description: src/worktree.rs link_shared_state uses 'if !dst.exists()' before symlinking. exists() follows symlinks, so a hostile pre-existing symlink at .balls/local / .balls/tasks / .balls/worktree inside an about-to-be-created bl worktree would be silently adopted. Low practical exposure (git worktree add just ran milliseconds earlier) but the primary ensure_tasks_symlink in store_init.rs uses a strict is_symlink() + error-if-non-symlink check. Mirror that pattern here.
title: Worktree claim: strict symlink check in link_shared_state
